sourceEvidence.label: G2 Pion v4.3.0 restricted-fork rung-one evidence

artifacts.title: G2 new-stack requirements and Pion v4.3.0 as-is rejection

assessment.summary: Among unmodified Pion ICE v4.3.0, a wrapper-only gateway, and an exact-base restricted fork, only the restricted-fork shape is suitable for a separate rung-two provenance and acquisition decision; nothing is implemented, selected, acquired, or runtime-verified.

nonNegotiables: Egress authorization is single-use and occurs after resolution immediately before every socket create, bind, connect, TURN TLS handshake, credential write, protocol write, or application-fragment write.

Ingress uses a fixed-size read and bounded minimal parse, then validates exact source, transaction, integrity, generation, TURN state, and content capability before state mutation, event creation, forwarding, or payload delivery.
TURN TLS authenticates the signed service identity and TLS profile before any TURN credential is sent.
Nomination yields only a one-use bounded pre-auth raw-path capability; exact AetherLink transcript confirmation atomically promotes that exact path to application-record use.
ICE reachability, Pion state, and TURN service authentication never authenticate an AetherLink endpoint.
Packet, protocol, rate, transaction, retransmit, task, goroutine, socket, TURN, generation, reassembly, event, and two-session process totals all have exact fail-before-allocation ceilings.
Event overflow sets an independent sticky terminal latch, discards nonterminal events, and closes without waiting for a consumer.
The Runtime channel cannot attach until a reliable ordered carrier and bounded fragmentation and reassembly format are selected and verified.
Secrets, raw candidates, addresses, certificate subjects, stable identities, and application data never enter diagnostics.
This analysis selects no library and authorizes no source acquisition, dependency installation, compilation, loading, socket, network, device, deployment, or Git action.
assumptions: No measured performance, memory, battery, NAT, or interoperability result is available.
The V1 release targets remain Android arm64-v8a API 26 through 36 and macOS 14 or newer on arm64.
The authenticated sealed relay remains the rollback path if direct traversal is unavailable.
The roadmap and handoff bytes are finalized in the ordered manifest and collection digest for this portfolio revision.

opportunities.title: Make bidirectional network, secure-session transition, resource, diagnostic, and lifetime ownership structural in a restricted Pion fork
opportunities.summary: Replace caller-convention controls with separate egress and ingress enforcement, authenticated TURN TLS identity, one-use pre-auth promotion, exact resource ceilings, a sticky terminal latch, secret-free diagnostics, and deadline shutdown.
opportunities.diagnosis: The reviewed upstream candidate exposes useful configuration, but internally owned socket, read-loop, diagnostic, callback, and shutdown paths prevent a wrapper from proving AetherLink's bidirectional policy, endpoint-transition, resource, and lifetime invariants.

evidence.claim: Pion ICE v4.3.0 commit 1e8716372f2bb52e45bf2a7172e4fb1004251c46 is rejected as-is because not all network paths share one immediate pre-I/O policy boundary, a remote ICE password enters diagnostics, callback queues lack a declared bound, and graceful close can wait indefinitely.
The V1 route model keeps endpoint identity and secure-session readiness above ICE and requires signed capability or lease context for service-mediated routes while preserving strict privacy floors.

The current G2 ladder separates requirements, source identity, static review, dependency closure, compile, no-network conformance, loopback, and controlled-network scopes; repository-owner authentication is not part of that technical ladder.
Current handoff state confirms a personal-project boundary and no retained, compiled, loaded, or executed Pion source, no selected library, and no opened socket or network rung.
Because unsafe paths are internally owned, wrapper-only configuration cannot make egress, ingress, event, diagnostic, and shutdown invariants non-bypassable.

desiredInvariants: Every egress side effect consumes one exact generation-purpose-transport-local-and-remote-tuple capability immediately before I/O.
Every UDP or TURN stream ingress path performs bounded read and parse, then admission before state mutation or delivery.
Inbound ICE Binding requests, TURN Data Indication and ChannelData, peer-reflexive tuples, consent, and selected-pair payloads are all covered by ingress admission.

TURN TLS validates the G1 trust source, signed service configuration, exact SNI and DNS-ID, trust digest, optional SPKI pin, TLS 1.3, stun.turn ALPN, expiry, and deadline before credential transmission.
A one-use pre-auth raw-path capability permits only bounded secure-session confirmation and carrier negotiation; exact transcript and both key confirmations atomically promote the exact path.
Consent loss, path change, restart, expiry, failure, or close revokes pre-auth and application capabilities.
All per-session limits include current, draining, and closing generations, and process totals include both possible sessions.
The normal event queue has an independent sticky terminal latch and can neither block close nor exceed its declared bound on overflow.
Pion remains an unordered unreliable datagram provider; Runtime attachment is blocked until reliable ordering and bounded fragmentation and reassembly are selected and verified.
Close revokes all resources and returns success or a terminal timeout within 2,500 milliseconds without waiting for consumer code.
Diagnostics expose only closed reason codes and saturating counts and contain no credentials, candidates, addresses, identities, payloads, certificate subjects, or keys.
Only the closed full-ICE UDP plus policy-gated TURN profile is available to the future bridge.

options.title: Retain unmodified Pion ICE v4.3.0
options.summary: Use upstream configuration and filters without source changes.

evidenceCoverage.rationale: The exact observed internal I/O, diagnostic, event, and shutdown gaps remain.
Reachability still lacks the exact AetherLink pre-auth-to-application promotion and TURN service-identity contract.
The roadmap's bounded G2 candidate requirements are not satisfied by the as-is shape.
The current no-source, no-selection, and no-runtime state remains unchanged.

tradeoffs.assessment: The bidirectional policy, TURN TLS, promotion, diagnostics, and resource gaps remain.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Repeat offline source review against the pinned commit; any unchanged gap keeps this option rejected.

tradeoffs.assessment: No new policy hop is introduced, but no candidate was compiled or measured.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Do not benchmark unless the security blockers are first removed.

tradeoffs.assessment: Queues and aggregate resources lack the closed ceilings required for a finite claim.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Require explicit queue, task, goroutine, socket, TURN, generation, and process bounds before testing.

tradeoffs.assessment: Blocked callbacks can prevent deterministic shutdown.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Close under a permanently blocked consumer must return within 2,500 milliseconds.

tradeoffs.assessment: Upstream tracking is simpler, but diagnostics cannot meet the product secrecy boundary.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Scan every log level with sentinel credentials, candidates, service identities, and certificate subjects.

tradeoffs.assessment: Using upstream unchanged minimizes patch work but remains inadmissible.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: No migration proceeds while this option remains rejected.

residualRisks: Internal egress and ingress paths remain outside a single owned policy boundary.
TURN credentials and endpoint-transition state lack the required structural gates.
Credentials can enter diagnostics and resource growth and shutdown remain unbounded.

workPackages: None; this option remains a baseline comparison.
acceptanceCriteria: Not applicable while the source-derived blockers remain.
implementationReadiness.rollback: Retain no Pion dependency and keep the authenticated sealed relay design.

options.title: Add a policy wrapper around unmodified upstream
options.summary: Restrict configuration, outbound adapters, and public callbacks without changing upstream internals.

evidenceCoverage.rationale: A wrapper narrows normal configuration but cannot own upstream-internal egress, ingress, queues, diagnostics, or close waits.
Outer route binding can reduce misuse, but cannot atomically promote an internally owned exact path.
Some feature and destination controls narrow, but the roadmap's structural and bounded requirements remain unproven.
The current no-source, no-selection, and no-runtime state remains unchanged.

tradeoffs.assessment: Configuration misuse narrows, but internal egress, ingress, TURN TLS, and event bypasses remain reachable.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Trace every create, bind, connect, write, read-loop, callback, diagnostic, and shutdown path from exact source.

tradeoffs.assessment: Outer checks add bounded work, but no build or benchmark exists.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure only after full internal path ownership is demonstrated.

tradeoffs.assessment: A bounded outer queue cannot bound upstream queues, tasks, goroutines, sockets, TURN state, or reassembly.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Flood every inner producer and inspect all aggregate ceilings while the outer consumer is blocked.

tradeoffs.assessment: An outer deadline can stop a caller wait but cannot prove internal resource revocation.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Inspect live workers, sockets, capabilities, and TURN state after wrapper timeout.

tradeoffs.assessment: Two policy and lifetime layers complicate incident reasoning.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Audit whether every failure code identifies the real owner without secret context.

tradeoffs.assessment: A wrapper reduces patch drift but cannot satisfy the acceptance gate.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Keep this as a rejected comparison unless a future exact upstream closes every inner gap.

residualRisks: Internally created and bound network paths can bypass the outer egress gateway.
Upstream read loops can mutate state before any outer ingress check.
The inner callback queue, secret diagnostics, TURN identity timing, and close wait remain outside wrapper ownership.

workPackages: None; preserve this option only as a regression comparison.
acceptanceCriteria: Every internal blocker must disappear in a newly reviewed exact upstream version before reconsideration.
implementationReadiness.rollback: Remove the wrapper and retain no networking candidate.

options.title: Maintain a minimal policy-owned restricted fork
options.summary: Require the exact base to place every network side effect, ingress mutation, path promotion, event, diagnostic, resource, and shutdown transition behind the closed AetherLink profile.

evidenceCoverage.rationale: The proposed patch shape would directly own the reported egress, ingress, diagnostic, event, and shutdown paths; implementation and validation remain future work.
The proposed one-use pre-auth and exact transcript promotion preserves the V1 endpoint-identity and route-authorization boundary.
The closed feature, resource, build, and progressive verification requirements map to the roadmap's G2 candidate gates.
The proposal deliberately leaves the current no-source, no-selection, no-runtime state unchanged.

tradeoffs.assessment: Bidirectional policy, TURN identity, endpoint promotion, and lifetime invariants become structural review points.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Offline path enumeration and mutation tests must prove every allowed and forbidden egress, ingress, promotion, and revocation path.

tradeoffs.assessment: Every I/O adds bounded policy work, and a reliable carrier plus fragmentation will add overhead; no cost is measured.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure nomination, consent, record throughput, reassembly, latency, and battery only after safety rungs pass.

tradeoffs.assessment: Closed packet, state, worker, socket, TURN, generation, reassembly, event, and process ceilings create a finite target.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Exercise every per-session and two-session aggregate ceiling concurrently and verify rejection before allocation.

tradeoffs.assessment: The sticky terminal latch and deadline close avoid dependence on consumer progress.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Block resolver, STUN, TURN TLS, ingress, carrier/reassembly, and consumers while asserting atomic revocation and the 2,500 ms total bound.

tradeoffs.assessment: The project assumes fork rebases, advisory triage, SBOM, reproducibility, carrier, and incident ownership.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Perform an upstream-update rehearsal and emergency direct-traversal disable drill before release.

tradeoffs.assessment: A narrow bridge, seven patch areas, and an additional reliable carrier contract require substantial gated work.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Keep upstream types out of the bridge and prove complete removal returns to sealed relay without protocol downgrade.

residualRisks: A missed internal create, bind, read, parse, or write path could still bypass policy until exhaustive source coverage proves completeness.
TURN TLS configuration or pre-auth promotion could be implemented inconsistently across direct and relay paths.
Reliable carrier and bounded fragmentation and reassembly are not selected and block Runtime attachment.
Go and gomobile toolchain drift can undermine reproducibility or ABI stability.
Fork maintenance can lag upstream security fixes.
No real NAT, performance, battery, socket, device, or interoperability behavior is known.

affectedComponents: future vendored Pion ICE restricted fork
future reliable ordered carrier and fragmentation layer
workPackages: Prepare a rung-two exact source identity and acquisition decision.
If separately permitted, acquire only the exact archive and record complete provenance.
Implement separate egress and ingress policy ownership across every source path.
Implement TURN TLS service identity and one-use pre-auth-to-application promotion.
Implement resource ceilings, sticky terminal latch, diagnostics, and deadline shutdown.
Select and validate the reliable ordered carrier and bounded fragmentation and reassembly contract.
Close dependency, license, SBOM, patch, symbol, and reproducibility evidence before compile-only integration.
acceptanceCriteria: All create, bind, connect, TLS, credential, and write operations consume the exact egress capability immediately before I/O.
All UDP and TURN stream reads pass bounded ingress admission before mutation, events, forwarding, or payload delivery.
TURN TLS sends no credential until the exact signed service identity and TLS profile pass.
Application records fail before exact AetherLink transcript confirmation and atomic one-use path promotion.
Consent loss, path change, restart, expiry, failure, and close revoke every path capability.
Every per-session and two-session aggregate limit rejects before allocation or insertion.
Event overflow sets the independent sticky terminal latch and never blocks or exceeds the declared queue bound.
Sentinel secrets, addresses, certificate subjects, and identities are absent from diagnostics.
Close returns by 2,500 milliseconds under every blocked dependency without waiting for a consumer.
Runtime attachment remains impossible until reliable carrier and bounded fragmentation and reassembly pass.
The two declared V1 architecture builds are reproducible with an exact minimal symbol surface.
migrationNotes: Do not expose upstream Pion types across the AetherLink bridge.
Keep direct traversal disabled until every G2 rung through the intended execution scope passes.
Do not interpret proposal text as implemented code or runtime evidence.
implementationReadiness.rollback: Disable and remove the unselected candidate, preserve the secure-session floor, and route through the authenticated sealed relay fallback.

opportunities.recommendation: Prepare the restricted-fork shape only as the candidate input to a separate rung-two provenance and acquisition decision; do not select, acquire, implement, compile, load, or execute it from this analysis.

openQuestions: Which exact official archive and signature or checksum chain would bind rung two?
Which G1 trust-root and signed-service-configuration bytes would bind TURN TLS identity?
Which reliable ordered carrier and bounded record-fragmentation and reassembly format can bridge Pion datagrams to RuntimeRawFrameBodyChannel?
Which exact x/mobile, gomobile, gobind, Android NDK, and Xcode revisions produce the closed V1 ABI?
How large is the exact fork diff after egress, ingress, TURN TLS, promotion, resource, event, diagnostic, and shutdown ownership are implemented?
Do later measured policy, carrier, reassembly, memory, latency, and battery costs fit the product budgets?
